article_items.title: NVIDIA Unveils Vera, the CPU for Agents
article_items.description: NVIDIA today announced that the world’s technology leaders are planning to adopt NVIDIA Vera, the first CPU built for AI agents.
article_items.excerpt: NVIDIA today announced that the world’s technology leaders are planning to adopt NVIDIA Vera, the first CPU built for AI agents.

article_items.description: This post demonstrates how to implement Open Authorization (OAuth) Code flow as an inbound authorization mechanism for MCP servers hosted on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway. By the end of this guide, you will have a production-ready setup where each AI assistant request is authenticated with a valid user identity token issued from your organization’s identity provider.

article_items.title: Nvidia unveils RTX Spark as most efficient PC chip ever built
article_items.description: Nvidia announces RTX Spark, its first complete consumer PC chip for laptops and mini-PCs, claiming it's the most efficient ever built. No benchmarks provided.
article_items.excerpt: Nvidia announces RTX Spark, its first complete consumer PC chip for laptops and mini-PCs, claiming it's the most efficient ever built. No benchmarks provided.
article_items.summary: Nvidia unveils RTX Spark as most efficient PC chip ever built The GPU giant is entering the consumer PC chip market this fall, putting a complete computing system on a single chip for laptops and mini-PCs. Software updates in 2026 added support for agentic AI frameworks like Hermes on the DGX Spark, improving memory management and local AI agent capabilities. This fall, the company will ship the RTX Spark, its first complete computing chip designed for laptops and mini-PCs, putting it in direct competition with Intel, AMD, Apple, and Qualcomm in the consumer PC silicon market. The company calls it “the most efficient PC chip ever built.” The catch: Nvidia hasn’t shared a single benchmark, statistic, or chart to support that claim. Nvidia announces RTX Spark, its first complete consumer PC chip for laptops and mini-PCs, claiming it's the most efficient ever built.

This fall, the company will ship the RTX Spark, its first complete computing chip designed for laptops and mini-PCs, putting it in direct competition with Intel, AMD, Apple, and Qualcomm in the consumer PC silicon.
That device, which began shipping in October 2025, combines a 20-core Arm CPU with a Blackwell GPU and delivers up to 1 petaFLOP of FP4 AI performance.
The chip is designed to compete with the best thin-and-light Windows laptops on the market, combining CPU and GPU capabilities into one package.
The company calls it “the most efficient PC chip ever built.” The catch: Nvidia hasn’t shared a single benchmark, statistic, or chart to support that claim.
The DGX Spark is priced between $3,000 and $4,000 and is available through ASUS, Dell, HP, Lenovo, and MSI.
Nvidia’s broader silicon ambitions The company already shipped the DGX Spark, a compact desktop AI system powered by the GB10 Grace Blackwell Superchip.
The company has positioned the device as the “world’s smallest AI supercomputer,” designed to let developers prototype and run sophisticated AI models without paying for cloud compute time.
The RTX Spark is the first in what Nvidia describes as a family of chips.

What we actually know about RTX Spark The RTX Spark represents Nvidia’s first foray into building a full system-on-chip for consumer PCs, not just a discrete GPU that slots into someone else’s machine.

rag_rules: Make the business move concrete: who is involved, what changed, and why now.
Tie funding, partnerships, or revenue to product direction or competitive pressure.
Avoid hype words unless the article gives evidence.
Lead with the actual product, model, or API name.

article_items.title: Meta's AI support chatbot made it ridiculously easy for hackers to take over Instagram accounts - Engadget
article_items.description: Meta says it's working on securing accounts that were affected by a hack.
article_items.excerpt: Meta says it's working on securing accounts that were affected by a hack.
article_items.summary: Back in December, Meta announced a new AI support assistant it promised would make the account recovery process "faster and simpler" for people who had been locked out of their Facebook or Instagram pages. The account, which hadn't posted since 2017, posted an AI-generated image that translates to "the White House is under Shiites' control," according to TMZ . The images and videos suggest that hackers were able to simply ask the AI support chatbot to change the email associated with their desired account and then request a password reset. Meta's AI support chatbot made it ridiculously easy for hackers to take over Instagram accounts The company says it's working on securing affected accounts. According to 404 Media , users on Telegram have been discussing the vulnerability since March.
article_items.what_happened: Back in December, Meta announced a new AI support assistant it promised would make the account recovery process "faster and simpler" for people who had been locked out of their Facebook or Instagram pages. The account, which hadn't posted since 2017, posted an AI-generated image that translates to "the White House is under Shiites' control," according to TMZ .
article_items.why_matters: The images and videos suggest that hackers were able to simply ask the AI support chatbot to change the email associated with their desired account and then request a password reset. Meta's AI support chatbot made it ridiculously easy for hackers to take over Instagram accounts The company says it's working on securing affected accounts.
article_items.what_to_watch: According to 404 Media , users on Telegram have been discussing the vulnerability since March.
key_points: The account, which hadn't posted since 2017, posted an AI-generated image that translates to "the White House is under Shiites' control," according to TMZ .
According to 404 Media , users on Telegram have been discussing the vulnerability since March.
The images and videos suggest that hackers were able to simply ask the AI support chatbot to change the email associated with their desired account and then request a password reset.
The exploit was flagged over the weekend by numerous security researchers on X.
Meta's AI support chatbot made it ridiculously easy for hackers to take over Instagram accounts The company says it's working on securing affected accounts.
Details about how to take over accounts, as well as screenshots and video showing the takeovers in action, were circulating widely on Telegram, the researchers said.

Back in December, Meta announced a new AI support assistant it promised would make the account recovery process "faster and simpler" for people who had been locked out of their Facebook or Instagram pages.
Other accounts that may have been caught up in the exploit include beauty retailer Sephora and a high-ranking Space Force official, according to 404 Media .
Meta has now addressed the issue, though it's unclear how many accounts were affected by the exploit before it was patched.

Though Meta didn't provide additional info on why its AI support tool would have such a gaping security vulnerability, it seems that hackers discovered the Meta chatbot relied on account holders' physical location to.
The now-patched exploit required hackers to use a VPN to show that their location matched the location of the person whose account they were targeting, according to Neowin .
According to security researchers, the AI tool made it ridiculously easy for hackers to take over the accounts, even if they were protected by two-factor authentication.
